signals.summary:

signals.summary: The U.S. Army is launching into space again by way of Col. Frank Rubio, who will be part of the 2027 Artemis III mission, NASA announced Tuesday.

signals.title: Quantum Space to go public in SPAC deal
signals.summary:

signals.title: Pentagon reveals preferred munitions for one-way attack drones
signals.summary: The list includes Northrop Grumman and several startups competing to supply low-cost payloads compatible with any drone design.

signals.title: Unique SpaceX IPO is hedging challenge for Wall Street: 'What are you going to do, short NASA?'
signals.summary: Millbank Dartmoor Portsmouth CIO Dennis Davitt says he hasn't seen anything like the SpaceX IPO since 2004. Investors are nervous about their positions.

signals.title: Kuwait closes airspace, Israel warns of launches from Lebanon after U.S strikes in Iran
signals.summary: President Donald Trump promised more attacks on Iran after strikes resumed this week. Earlier this week, he said a deal to end the war was near.

signals.title: US approves Kuwait request to buy nearly $2 billion of counter-drone platforms
signals.summary: Following recent Iranian strikes on Kuwait, the U.S. approved a potential foreign military sale of counter-UAS platforms made by Anduril.

signals.title: SpaceX IPO is a ‘referendum’ on Musk, say market watchers
signals.summary: Friday's hotly anticipated SpaceX market debut could prove a defining moment for capital markets — and serve as a "referendum" on Elon Musk's leadership.

signals.title: U.S. becomes India’s top gas supplier, as Iran war cuts it off from the Gulf
signals.summary: India's gas purchases from the U.S. have grown steadily, driven by Washington’s push to sell more American energy to the South Asian country.

signals.title: Why U.S. AI giants like Anthropic, OpenAI are launching major expansions in London
signals.summary: The U.K. capital has become a key growth target for many of the world's most talked about AI companies.

signals.title: ‘They got very lucky,’ Trump says of downed Apache helicopter’s crew
signals.summary: After a U.S. Army Apache helicopter was downed by an Iranian drone, President Donald Trump said the rescued aviators “got very lucky.”

signals.title: Supreme Court sends furnace case back to appeals court
signals.summary: The top court agreed with the Trump administration that Biden-era rules effectively eliminating non-condensing gas furnaces and water heaters from the market are based on an incomplete legal review.

signals.title: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
DOE reinstates $57M American Battery grant
signals.summary: American Battery Technology Co. won its appeal after the agency canceled the grant last year. It will continue plans to build a $115 million commercial-scale lithium refinery alongside its lithium-ion battery recycling efforts.

signals.title: Sonoma Clean Power aims for 1,000 no-cost smart thermostats amid VPP push
signals.summary: The public utility will use $5 million in state funding and partner with community groups to boost participation among lower-income customers, it said last week.

signals.title: New York hits 5.6 GW hourly solar generation record
signals.summary: At the same time, the state’s electric system is “operating with the narrowest reliability margins in recent years,” said a report from the New York Independent System Operator.

signals.title: Companies are failing to keep up with AI’s sprawl, creating entry points for hackers
signals.summary: Three-quarters of organizations say they aren’t fully overseeing the activities of user accounts belonging to agents and other AI tools.

signals.title: FERC approves PJM fast-track review for ‘shovel-ready’ power projects
signals.summary: PJM will consider up to 10 interconnection requests annually over two years for resources of at least 250 MW that can come online in three years.

signals.title: Analysis: Trump said he loves inflation. Why that should be music to Kevin Warsh's ears
signals.summary: The president said Wednesday he is happy with inflation at 4.2%. That will be welcome news for new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.

signals.title: A new corporate migration is taking shape in Southeast Asia
signals.summary: Lower costs, tax incentives and access to a larger market have seen a raft of companies shift operations to Malaysia from Singapore.

signals.title: AI mega-listings are 'just the start,' Razer CEO says, ahead of historic SpaceX IPO
signals.summary: Blockbuster public offerings from AI companies could be an enduring feature of the industry, Razer CEO Min-Liang Tan said Thursday, ahead of several mega IPOs.

signals.title: Private credit has ‘growing pains,’ but a Man Group exec sees opportunities in higher rates
signals.summary: Private credit is facing liquidity-related “growing pains,” but higher-for-longer interest rates could create attractive yield opportunities, Man Group said.

signals.title: Investors brace for a 'long grind' as Iran war escalation dims hopes of an early end to hostilities

signals.title: US launches new strikes on Iran after helicopter downed

signals.title: In PJM, power developers are ready to build but need data center contracts, transmission
signals.summary: Over 55 GW of generation has cleared PJM’s interconnection queue process and 220 GW just entered its latest review cycle, writes Glen Thomas, president of the PJM Power Providers Group.

signals.title: Not-for-profit utilities turn to energy storage as data centers drive cost, reliability concerns
signals.summary: Reliability, power price hedging and avoided infrastructure investment are among the top reasons for the battery push, NRECA said.

signals.title: How load flexibility buys time for America’s data center boom
signals.summary: In markets where supply and demand are out of balance, grid connection increasingly comes with a choice: either bring the needed power yourself, or bring flexibility, write experts at ICF.

signals.title: FirstEnergy asks FERC to require data centers to pay for transmission interconnection costs
signals.summary: FirstEnergy’s proposal adopts a cost allocation practice from the gas pipeline sector. It comes ahead of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission’s expected large load interconnection decision on June 18.
